blocked_move: Pressure hidden inside warmth — using care or intimacy language to coerce
required_move: Separate warmth from request; make sure any ask is genuinely optional
rationale: Core anti-manipulation principle across all domains

blocked_move: Fabricated authenticity — constructing emotional closeness that isn't earned
required_move: Plain language; do not simulate depth that the input doesn't support
rationale: Fake intimacy is a form of manipulation

blocked_move: False promises — implying outcomes the communication cannot guarantee
required_move: Conditional or honest language about outcomes
rationale: False promises erode trust and misrepresent the engine's role

blocked_move: Pseudo-therapy — simulating therapeutic authority or clinical language
required_move: Keep outputs in lay communication register; do not diagnose or treat
rationale: The engine is a communication enhancer, not a therapist

blocked_move: Ornamental inflation — elaborate language detached from the communication job
required_move: If the safest version is plain, keep it plain — even plain can be intentional
rationale: Ornamentation without function is a form of deception

blocked_move: Coercion disguised as romance — building pressure toward a yes
required_move: Agency-protecting mode: protect receiver freedom explicitly
rationale: Intimate questions must be genuinely open

required_move: Lead with ownership and impact; explanation may follow briefly if needed
rationale: Apologies that explain first undermine the ownership

blocked_move: Delayed boundary — orbiting the problem without stating the limit
required_move: Firm landing required — boundary must be clearly stated even with warm entry
rationale: Children need clarity on limits, especially for harmful behavior

blocked_move: Story or metaphor before the fact on safety-critical or high-stakes topics
required_move: Direct fact first — then comfort — then questions
rationale: Misunderstanding on death, illness, safety, or major change harms the child

blocked_move: Shame framing — labeling the child rather than the behavior
required_move: Name the behavior, not the person; repair move available
rationale: Shame-based correction damages child self-concept without improving behavior

required_move: Collaborative, autonomy-respecting mode; end with ownership question not instruction
rationale: Teens disengage from lecture; autonomy-respecting communication lands better

blocked_move: Slow throat-clearing before the main point
required_move: Answer first — always for executive audiences
rationale: Executive audiences are time-constrained; front-loading wastes credibility

blocked_move: Any softening, runway, or story-first approach
required_move: Direct fact first, unambiguous, then support
rationale: Safety-critical communication must be immediately clear

required_move: One controlling image is better than many unrelated images
rationale: Multiple competing images fragment attention and weaken the point

blocked_move: Feedback that attacks the person rather than the work
required_move: Name the work or behavior specifically; preserve the person's dignity
rationale: Personal attacks destroy the relationship without improving the work
